data.title: IREN is the top performing application software stock
data.description:
Information technology index was the second-best-performing sector in 2025, as it rose +25% YTD, while its accompanying State Street Technology Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLK) is +26.14%.

As we look at the subsectors of the IT index, below is a list of the top 10 application software stocks ranked by their YTD performance. The list includes each stock’s Quant ratings.

The list is topped by IREN (IREN), with a YTD performance of +327.49%. Bitmine Immersion Technologies (BMNR) and Cipher Mining (CIFR) follow close behind, with D-Wave Quantum (QBTS) and Hut 8 (HUT) rounding out the top five.

Notably, Hut 8 (HUT) is the only stock in the top ten with a “Strong Buy” Quant Rating of 4.94. The remaining stocks on the list carry “Hold” ratings, with Palantir Technologies (PLTR) and AppLovin (APP) among the notable names with YTD gains exceeding 100%.

Here is the list:

  1. IREN (IREN), YTD perf: +327.49%, Quant Rating: Hold 3.48

  2. Bitmine Immersion Technologies (BMNR), YTD perf: +276.28%, Quant Rating: Hold 3.48

  3. Cipher Mining (CIFR), YTD perf: +249.57%, Quant Rating: Hold 3.48

  4. D-Wave Quantum (QBTS), YTD perf: +227.62%, Quant Rating: Hold 3.43

  5. Hut 8 (HUT), YTD perf: +157.69%, Quant Rating: Strong Buy 4.94

  6. Palantir Technologies (PLTR), YTD perf: +156.74%, Quant Rating: Hold 3.48

  7. AppLovin (APP), YTD perf: +124.65%, Quant Rating: Hold 3.45

  8. TeraWulf (WULF), YTD perf: +117.49%, Quant Rating: Hold 3.36

  9. Unity Software (U), YTD perf: +102.23%, Quant Rating: Hold 3.14

  10. InterDigital, Inc. (IDCC), YTD perf: +69.49%, Quant Rating: Hold 3.08

Here are some technology sector-focused exchange-traded funds: (VGT), (XLK), (IYW), (FTEC), (IXN), and (RSPT).

data.title: Sable Offshore sinks as environmental groups file emergency lawsuit to stop pipeline restart
data.description:
Sable Offshore (SOC) -12.9% in Friday's trading after the Environmental Defense Center and Center for Biological Diversity filed an emergency lawsuit seeking to block the Trump administration's approval this week of the company's plan to restart oil production at the Las Flores pipeline system off the coast of Santa Barbara, California.

"Restarting the pipeline system would not only invite another oil disaster on the Central Coast, but all but ensure it. The pipeline system has already ruptured once, and the underlying cause of that rupture has not been corrected," according to the petition for review and emergency motion for a stay filed late on December 24 at the Ninth Circuit U.S. Court of Appeals.

The petition claims the U.S. Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration bypassed the requirement for public notice, opportunity for public participation, environmental review under the National Environmental Policy Act and other conditions required under the Pipeline Safety Act, and called the environmental approval a "rushed, legally dubious, and politicized process."

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Defense stocks in play, FOMC minutes, and opening 2026 trades
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Monday - December 29

  • Volatility watch - Energy Fuels (UUUU) and Applied Digital (APLD) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Hycroft Mining Holding (HYMC), Amicus (FOLD), and Charlotte's Web (CWBHF). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include CVR Energy (CVI), Tile Shop (TTSH), and Lamb Weston (LW).
  • Spotlight on defense - President Trump plans to meet with defense contractors next week to discuss production schedules of equipment. The meeting could impact defense stocks such as Lockheed Martin (LMT), RTX (RTX), Northrop Grumman (NOC), General Dynamics (GD), and Boeing (BA).
  • 10:00 a.m. The November Pending Home Sales report will be released by the National Association of Realtors.
  • 10:30 a.m. The Dallas Fed Manufacturing Survey will be released.

 

Tuesday - December 30

  • All day - The Nevada Gaming Commission and Board is expected to release the gaming win report for November sometime during the week. The overall report will be of interest to Caesars Entertainment (CZR), MGM Resorts (MGM), Boyd Gaming (BYD), Wynn Resorts (WYNN), VICI Properties (VICI), Full House Resorts (FLL), Bally's Corporation (BALY), and Red Rock Resorts (RRR). In particular, the Las Vegas Strip numbers for the month will be closely watched for the impact of the Formula One race weekend.
  • 9:00 a.m. The S&P/Case-Shiller House Price Index will be released.
  • 10:30 a.m. The Dallas Fed Texas Retail Outlook Survey will be released.
  • 2:00 p.m. The Federal Reserve will release the minutes from its last meeting.

 

Wednesday - December 31

  • All day - The stock market slightly underperforms on the last trading day of the year on a historic basis due in part to tax-loss harvesting trades settling and portfolio window dressing.
  • All day - Six Flags Entertainment Corporation (FUN) is scheduled to open and operate its first theme park outside the U.S. next week in a notable development for the travel and leisure sector. Six Flags Qiddiya City in Saudi Arabia will open as the flagship theme park of Saudi Arabia's Qiddiya City giga-project.
  • 1:00 p.m. The U.S. stock markets will close early for observance of the New Year's Day holiday.

 

Thursday - January 1

  • All day - U.S. stock markets will be closed for observance of the New Year's Day holiday.

 

Friday - January 2

  • Data watch - Key data reports are due to arrive during the first few days of the month, including updates on deliveries from Tesla (TSLA), NIO (NIO), XPeng (XPEV), Rivian Automotive (RIVN), Li Auto (LI), ZEEKR Intelligent (GELYF), Polestar Automotive (PSNY), and Lucid Group (LCID). Quarterly sales reports from General Motors (GM), Ford Motor (F), and Toyota (TM) will also be closely watched. Other reports of high interest to investors will be the monthly Macau gross gaming revenue release, the monthly update on Class 8 truck orders, firearm background check data from the FBI, and discount broker DARTs reports.

 

Seeking Alpha editor Josh Fineman contributed to this article.

data.title: CyberArk/Palo Alto Networks, Dayforce/Thoma Bravo among good bets for deal close
data.description:
Palo Alto Networks' (PANW) $25 billion purchase of CyberArk (CYBR) and Thoma Bravo's planned  $12.3 billion acquisition of Dayforce are among the deals likely to close in the new year.

"Thoma Bravo is a really well-known PE firm that has a good record of closing deals,"  Roy Behren, co-chief investment officer at Westchester Capital, said in an interview with Seeking Alpha earlier this month.

Other deal spreads that Behren likes include IBM's (IBM) planned $11 billion purchase of Confluent (CFLT), Silver Lake's $55 billion acquisition of Electronic Arts (EA), and Abbot's (ABT) $21 billion takeover of Exact Sciences (EXAS).

"The opportunities are out there," Behren said. "You have to avoid the minefields. There are so many high-quality deals out there."

Behren also likes Anglo American's (AAUKF) $23 billion purchase of Teck Resources (TECK).

One M&A situation that Behren is staying away from is Kimberly-Clark's (KMB) planned  more than $40 billion acquisition of Kenvue (KVUE). 

"People are worried about the Tylenol exposure," Behren explained. "People don't want to invest in Kenvue and have some verdict go against them." 

Westchester is also avoiding Alcon's (ALC) planned purchase of STAAR Surgical (STAA), which has had a lot of opposition from shareholders.

"It's a coin flip, and we don't need to wager on a coin flip," Behren said. "We are not going to stretch. There is so much inventory of transactions we can find to invest in."

Overall, 2025 has been a good year for M&A due to more relaxed regulations and a speedier approval process under the Trump administration, according to Behren. 

"The regulators are just so much more business-friendly," Behren added. "We are seeing regulatory reviews in a quick manner. The regulatory path has been much clearer than it has been in many, many years.”

Worldwide mergers and acquisitions increased by almost 50% from 2024 to $4.5 trillion in 2025, according to data from the London Stock Exchange Group, the  Financial Times reported on Friday. It's the second-highest total of dealmaking in more than 40 years, only outdone by 2021. 

Behren expects that M&A will continue to pick up in the new year due to potentially lower interest rates, plentiful money from both banks and private sources and the friendly regulatory environment.

“We expect to see more transactions in tech, healthcare, and even industrial companies as automation continues to play an important role in cost-savings and increasing margins," Behren said.

data.title: Stocks waver post-Christmas, while precious metals hit new record highs
data.description:
Wall Street wavered on Friday, amid small moves and low trading volume post the Christmas holiday. Precious metals grabbed the spotlight as they extended their meteoric rise this year.

The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) slipped -0.04% to 23,603.13 points. The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) declined -0.12% to 6,923.46 points, while the blue-chip Dow (DJI) fell -0.27% to 48,600.64 points.

Traders are returning from a truncated Christmas Eve session on Wednesday and the holiday on Thursday. A potential Santa Claus rally is in the spotlight - a historical trend that has seen the S&P (SP500) post gains during the last five trading days of the year and the first two of the next year. The rally got off to a promising start on Wednesday, with the benchmark gauge notching a record close. 

"Christmas Hangover? Usually not in the stock market. In the 39 years since 1953, when the market has been open on 12/26, it has only declined six times," Bespoke Investment Group noted on X. See the chart they shared below:

Precious metals were in focus after silver, gold, and platinum scaled new all-time highs. 

Spot silver (XAGUSD:CUR) added as much as +2.4% to hit $76.53/oz. Spot gold (XAUUSD:CUR) increased as much as +1.5% to a record $4,549.82/oz, while spot platinum (XPTUSD:CUR) jumped as much as +10.3% to a peak of $2,493.20.

"Mag Metals. If Platinum, Palladium, and Silver were S&P 500 stocks, they would be the top three performers since the close on 12/10. Gold would rank 43rd," Bespoke observed.

U.S. Treasury yields showed a mixed picture. The shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) moved lower to 3.50%. Meanwhile, longer-dated yields climbed, with the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rising to 4.14% and the U.S. 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) advancing to 4.83%.

Top gainers included Freeport-McMoRan (FCX) +2.27%, Target (TGT) +1.91%, and Nvidia (NVDA) +1.49%. Decliners included Moderna (MRNA) -4.08%, Texas Pacific Land (TPL) -3.32%, and Brown-Forman (BF.B) -2.90%.

data.title: Technology sector soars 25% in 2025, with Lumentum as top YTD performer
data.description:
Heavyweight information technology stocks have emerged as the second-best-performing sector in 2025, helping to drive the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500) to another year of double-digit gains.

The S&P 500 IT sector is +25% YTD, while its accompanying State Street Technology Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLK) is +26.14%.

The advance underscores the popularity of the sector, as investors continued to pour money into technology stocks despite refrains of lofty valuations and market bubbles. The artificial intelligence trade thrived once again in 2025 as demand for AI products and the chips that power them showed no signs of slowing. 

Seeking Alpha took a look at the technology sector and identified the top 10 YTD performers. Lumentum (LITE) grabbed the number one spot with a whopping +372% gain. Note that SA looked at only mid-cap companies and larger.

Here is the list:

  1. Lumentum (LITE), Quant Rating: Strong Buy 4.99, +371.61% YTD.
  2. IREN (IREN), Quant Rating: Hold 3.48, +327.49% YTD.
  3. Viasat (VSAT), Quant Rating: Strong Buy 4.89, +313.04% YTD.
  4. Bitmine Immersion Technologies (BMNR), Quant Rating: Hold 3.48, +276.28% YTD.
  5. Ondas (ONDS), Quant Rating: Hold 3.49, +256.64% YTD.

data.title: X3 Holdings announces share consolidation
data.description:
• X3 Holdings (XTKG) on Friday said it will effect a share consolidation of its ordinary shares at a ratio of 1-for-6.
  • Immediately following the share consolidation, the par value of the company's ordinary shares will be reduced from $48.00 to $0.00003 per share by cancelling the paid-up share capital to the extent of $47.99997 per share by way of a reduction of capital, both of which will become effective on December 30, 2025.
  • Upon market opening on December 30, the company's Class A ordinary shares will continue to be traded on Nasdaq under the symbol "XTKG"
  • XTKG is -23.23% to $0.1259.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Intelligent Living to implement 1-for-10 reverse stock split to meet Nasdaq bid rule
data.description:
• Intelligent Living Application Group (ILAG) on Friday said its board has approved a 1-for-10 share consolidation, or reverse stock split, following shareholder authorization earlier this month.
  • The company said the reverse split will take effect on December 30, 2025, with its ordinary shares trading on a post-consolidation basis on Nasdaq under the same symbol, ILAG.
  • The move is intended to bring the company into compliance with Nasdaq’s minimum bid price requirement.
  • Under the consolidation, Intelligent Living’s outstanding ordinary shares will be reduced to about 2.08 million from 20.77 million, while the par value will increase to $0.001 from $0.0001.
  • The company’s Series A preferred shares will be reduced to about 200,000 from 2 million, and Series B preferred shares to about 1 million from 10 million, with the same increase in par value.
  • Fractional shares will be rounded up, and shareholders’ proportional ownership and voting power will remain substantially unchanged.
  • ILAG is -3.66% to $0.4046.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: XPeng's Middle East expansion lifts Chinese EV names
data.description:
Xpeng (XPEV) is launching its popular models in the Middle East and Africa, partnering with a subsidiary of Almana Group as the company’s exclusive distributor in Qatar, and Axess Limited in the East African nation of Mauritius.

The Chinese EV maker will enter the Qatari market with the G6 and G9 SUVs and, later with the P7+ sedan. Xpeng (XPEV) also showcased products from its Adridge flying car subsidiary at the product launch in Doha.

“Qatar will serve as a vital gateway for us to connect with global users, showcase cutting-edge smart technologies, and deliver premium mobility experiences,” said Xpeng’s Middle East and Africa general manager, Wang Ke.

The company also has an “experience center” in Abu Dhabi, showroom and service center in Egypt, and accessories warehouse in Dubai.

Xpeng’s (XPEV) Middle East ambition aims to close the gap with BYD, the largest Chinese EV brand in MENA, with both competing for a large chunk of the untapped Middle East market.

According to data from AlixPartners, Chinese EVs are forecasted to achieve a 34% market share in the oil-rich region by 2030, up from 10% in 2024, making the Middle East/Africa with the highest share of Chinese vehicles outside of China, Russia and Belarus.  Additionally, nearly three-quarters of Middle East EV car buyers place a high level of trust in cars made in China, more than double the confidence placed on U.S.-made EVs.

Xpeng’s (XPEV) Middle East expansion is boosting shares in the Chinese EV category with Xpeng (XPEV) up 8%, NIO (NIO) and Li Auto (LI) up 3% and BYD (BYDDY) (BYDDF) trading with a gain of 1.4%.
